description:
You are acting as a build-pipeline engineer who has to create and register a new, **dedicated** SSH key-pair that will later be used by automated jobs to upload release artifacts to an internal repository.

  Perform the following steps from a Linux terminal.  All paths and file names *must* be **exactly** as written (including upper/lower-case).  The automated checker will fail if anything differs.

  1. Create the directory “/home/user/build-artifacts/keys”.  
     • Make sure the directory permissions are 0700 (rwx------).

  2. Inside that directory, generate an **ed25519** SSH key-pair with **no pass-phrase**.  
     • File name (no extension):  
       /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519  
     • Comment (the text that appears at the end of the public key line):  
       build-engineer@artifact-repo  
     • This will automatically create two files:  
       - /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519        (private key)  
       - /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519.pub    (public key)

  3. Permissions:  
     • Private key: 0600  
     • Public  key: 0644

  4. Maintain an **authorized_keys** style file for internal services:  
     • Append the newly generated *public* key line (exactly as produced by ssh-keygen) to  
       /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/authorized_keys  
       ‑ Create the file if it does not yet exist.  
     • The authorized_keys file itself must have permission 0600.

  5. Create a human-readable audit log **/home/user/build-artifacts/ssh_key_creation.log** that contains exactly six (6) lines, in the precise order and format shown below (replace the angle-bracket placeholders with real values obtained during execution):

     Line-1: Key generation timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS (UTC)  
     Line-2: Key file (private): /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519  
     Line-3: Key file (public): /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519.pub  
     Line-4: Key fingerprint: <algorithm> <fingerprint> <comment>  
             (Use the exact output of: ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519.pub)  
     Line-5: Public key line: <the full, single-line “ssh-ed25519 … build-engineer@artifact-repo” text>  
     Line-6: END

     Formatting rules:  
     • Exactly one space follows each colon.  
     • No extra spaces or blank lines anywhere.  
     • The file ends with a single trailing newline.

  6. When everything is complete, the directory tree “/home/user/build-artifacts” should look like this (permissions in parentheses):

       build-artifacts (drwx------)
       ├── keys (drwx------)
       │   ├── artifact_sync_ed25519        (-rw-------)
       │   ├── artifact_sync_ed25519.pub    (-rw-r--r--)
       │   └── authorized_keys              (-rw-------)
       └── ssh_key_creation.log             (-rw-r--r--)

  7. Do **not** delete any intermediate files you create; the test harness will inspect them.

  Verification checklist the grader will follow:
     • Paths and permissions match the specification.  
     • The public key line in both “.pub” and “authorized_keys” ends with “build-engineer@artifact-repo”.  
     • The log file exists, has exactly six lines, follows the given literal text template, and its fingerprint & public-key values correlate with the generated key.  
     • The directory hierarchy and file names are correct and reside under /home/user.

  Hint: typical utilities you will need are `mkdir`, `chmod`, `ssh-keygen`, `cat`, `echo`, and shell redirection operators (`>`, `>>`).

  Important: the automated tests rely *strictly* on the spelling, letter-case and spacing described above.  Double-check before you finish.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth information used by the automated evaluation script.
    The grader will:

    1. Verify the existence of:
       /home/user/build-artifacts/keys
       /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519
       /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519.pub
       /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/authorized_keys
       /home/user/build-artifacts/ssh_key_creation.log

    2. Check permissions:
       - keys directory: 0700
       - artifact_sync_ed25519:   0600
       - artifact_sync_ed25519.pub: 0644
       - authorized_keys: 0600
       - ssh_key_creation.log: 0644 or 0644/0600 is acceptable (readable)

    3. Read /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519.pub
       • Verify it begins with "ssh-ed25519 AAAA" and ends with "build-engineer@artifact-repo\n"

    4. Ensure authorized_keys contains the identical public key line (byte-for-byte).

    5. Compute fingerprint with `ssh-keygen -lf` and confirm that:
       • Line-4 of ssh_key_creation.log == "Key fingerprint: " + (exact output of ssh-keygen -lf …)

    6. Validate log format:
       • Exactly 6 lines.
       • Line-1 matches regex:
           ^Key generation timestamp: \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} \(UTC\)$
       • Line-2 == "Key file (private): /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519"
       • Line-3 == "Key file (public): /home/user/build-artifacts/keys/artifact_sync_ed25519.pub"
       • Line-4 regex:
           ^Key fingerprint: (ED25519|SHA256) [A-Za-z0-9+/=]+ build-engineer@artifact-repo$
           (OpenSSH 8+ prints something like "256 SHA256:xxxxxxxx build-engineer@artifact-repo", but we accept both formats.)
       • Line-5 begins with "Public key line: ssh-ed25519 " and ends with "build-engineer@artifact-repo"
       • Line-6 == "END"

    7. Confirm final newline at end of log file.
  -->
name: task_000000_4297237d
